Pain and Fever.notes: Used for mild pain and fever. Best taken after food to protect your stomach. Take it every 4 to 6 hours if needed. PRECAUTION: Do not take more than 8 tablets in 24 hours.
Used for pain and swelling. It is VERY important to take this AFTER a meal. PRECAUTION: Avoid if you have asthma or stomach ulcers.
A strong medicine for joint or muscle pain. Best taken after a full meal. Take 1 tablet twice a day. PRECAUTION: Can cause dizziness. Do not take on an empty stomach.

Stomach and Digestion.notes: Used to stop loose motions or diarrhea. Take one capsule after each loose motion. Drink plenty of water or ORS to stay hydrated. PRECAUTION: Stop taking it as soon as your motions are normal.
Used for stomach gas and acidity. Best taken 30 minutes before a meal. PRECAUTION: If you are taking antacid liquids, wait 2 hours before taking this tablet.

Acidity.notes: Used for heartburn and acidity. It works best if taken on an EMPTY stomach, usually 30 minutes before breakfast. PRECAUTION: Swallow the capsule whole; do not crush it.
Helps reduce stomach acid. Best taken in the morning before eating anything. PRECAUTION: If you miss a dose, take it as soon as you remember.

Allergy and Cold.notes: Used for runny nose and sneezing. Best taken at BEDTIME because it can make you feel very sleepy. PRECAUTION: Do not drive after taking this.
Helps with allergy symptoms. Can be taken with or without food. PRECAUTION: Avoid drinking alcohol while taking this as it increases sleepiness.

Muscle and Joint.notes: Used to relax tight muscles. It is VERY important to take this AFTER a meal. PRECAUTION: May cause mild drowsiness or dark-colored urine, which is normal.

Blood Pressure and Heart.notes: Used to lower high blood pressure. Best taken at the same time every day. PRECAUTION: Do not stop taking this suddenly without talking to a doctor.
Helps the heart beat more slowly and with less force. Best taken before a meal or at bedtime. PRECAUTION: May make you feel sleepy or dizzy at first.

Diabetes.notes: Helps control blood sugar levels. It is best to take this WITH or AFTER a meal to reduce stomach upset. PRECAUTION: Avoid drinking alcohol while on this medicine.

Infections.notes: An antibiotic used to kill bacteria. Take it at the start of a meal. PRECAUTION: You MUST finish the whole course, even if you feel better.
Used for throat or chest infections. Best taken 1 hour before or 2 hours after food. PRECAUTION: Do not take antacids at the same time.

Vitamins.notes: Helps with energy and nerve health. Best taken in the morning after breakfast. PRECAUTION: It may turn your urine bright yellow, which is normal.
